programme:
# Functional programme — Altamont & Rees

## 1. Context and headcount

- **Client**: Altamont & Rees, commercial-law partnership, City of London (Gracechurch Street, two floors, south façade onto courtyard / north façade onto street).
- **Headcount**: 140 today (90 lawyers + 30 paralegals + 20 support) → **160 FTE at 24 months**. Assumed split at horizon: ~20 named partners, ~90 associates, 30 paralegals, 20 support `[TO VERIFY: exact partner / associate ratio]`.
- **Presence policy**: 5/0 for partners and associates, 4/1 for paralegals, 5/0 for support. This is a **near-full-onsite** legal brief, not a hybrid brief.
- **Flex-ratio policy**: assigned seating throughout, **ratio ≈ 1.0 seats/FTE**. This is a deliberate counter-trend position against the 2024–2025 industry drift (48 % of organisations now target 1.01–1.49 pers/seat, legal sector median 0.50–0.65) and should be argued as such, not apologised for.
- **Design intent retained from the brief**:
  - "Named partners have private offices" / "associates share 2-to-a-room" / "paralegals in neighbourhoods"
  - "Zero open-plan exposure of confidential matter"
  - "Discreet client path from reception to partners"
  - "Deep reading in 3–6 h blocks" as the dominant work mode
  - "Library with bound reports, wine cellar and tasting kitchen for client dinners"
  - "Depositions in boardrooms" (plural)

## 2. Functional programme

| Typology | Count | Unit (m²) | Total (m²) | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Private partner office | 20 | 14 | 280 | Brief: named partners in private offices. 14 m² supports desk + 2 guest chairs + bookcase. `[TO VERIFY: exact named-partner count]` |
| Shared associate office (2p) | 45 | 18 | 810 | Brief: associates 2-to-a-room. 90 associates ÷ 2. |
| Paralegal neighbourhood seat | 30 | 8 | 240 | Brief: paralegals in neighbourhoods. 4/1 policy would give 0.90–0.95 seats/FTE, but held at 1.0 to avoid confidential-matter exposure at hot desks. |
| Support / secretarial workstation | 20 | 9 | 180 | 20 support, 5/0, semi-enclosed clusters adjacent to partner offices (PA pattern). `[TO VERIFY: dedicated PAs vs pool]` |
| Focus room (1–2p) | 6 | 5 | 30 | Top of 2–4 / 100 FTE band; deep-reading is the dominant declared mode. |
| Phone booth | 10 | 1.5 | 15 | Low end of 6–10 / 100 FTE; most confidential calls happen inside offices. |
| Huddle room (2–4p) | 6 | 8 | 48 | Case-team stand-ups, fully enclosed, DnT,A ≥ 40 dB. |
| Medium meeting room (6–8p) | 5 | 22 | 110 | Frequent confidential case-team meetings. |
| Boardroom / deposition room | 3 | 40 | 120 | Brief: depositions in boardrooms (plural); top of 1–2 / 100 FTE band, DnT,A ≥ 45 dB. |
| Client reception / waiting | 1 | 60 | 60 | Client-facing reception + discreet path; 8–10 seated visitors + concierge. |
| Library (bound reports) | 1 | 80 | 80 | ~400 linear m of shelving + 6 reading seats. |
| Wine cellar | 1 | 15 | 15 | Climate-controlled, off client path. `[TO VERIFY: target bottle capacity]` |
| Tasting kitchen / private dining | 1 | 50 | 50 | Seats 10–14 + open kitchen + sommelier station, adjacent to cellar. |
| Café / staff restaurant | 1 | 120 | 120 | 160 FTE at 5/0; ~0.75 m²/FTE at 50 % peak. `[TO VERIFY: external canteen option]` |
| Print / copy / secure doc room | 3 | 10 | 30 | 1 per floor + 1 secure doc room with shredder, locked output tray. |
| Locker bank | — | — | 10 | Paralegals only (30 lockers). |
| Reception cloakroom | 1 | 15 | 15 | Visitor + staff coats. |
| Back-office / MDF / IT | 2 | 20 | 40 | One per floor; secure comms for matter management. |
| Archives / strongroom | 1 | 40 | 40 | Fire-rated, access-controlled; separate from active library. `[TO VERIFY: 24-month archive volume — could push to 60–100 m²]` |
| Storage / cleaning / utility | 2 | 10 | 20 | 1 per floor. |
| PMR / accessible WC + standard WC | 2 | 25 | 50 | 1 block per floor; accessible WC 1.50 × 2.10 m minimum. |

**Sub-total (before circulation): 2 263 m²**
Circulation at 10 %: +226 m²
**Programme total: ~2 489 m²** vs **2 400 m² available → overshoots by ~90 m² (3.7 %)**.

## 3. Surface summary

- **Total programme surface**: **~2 489 m²** against **2 400 m²** usable → envelope breach **~90 m²**.
- **Split across the four categories**:
  - Individual work: **1 555 m² = 62 %** (above the 45–55 % band, driven by cellular legal typology)
  - Collaboration: **278 m² = 11 %** (below the 20–30 % band; meetings happen inside lawyer offices)
  - Support & amenity: **430 m² = 17 %** (within the 15–25 % band)
  - Circulation: **~226 m² = 10 %** (at the low end; cellular plans typically land at 14–15 %)
- **Implied NIA/FTE at 24-month horizon**: **15.0 m²/FTE** — top of the activity-based band (12–15) and entering premium-HQ territory (15–18). Consistent with a City law firm; below this ratio the programme does not fit.
- **Envelope check**: tight. The programme must be value-engineered by ~90 m² (see §6).

## 4. Industry positioning

Leesman 2024 baseline: **Lmi = 69.5**, home score **H-Lmi = 79.5** — a persistent 10-point gap. For a 5-days-on-site lawyer policy, the office must beat the home study on focus, acoustics and comfort, or attendance will be resented.

Relevant pain points (% satisfied, Leesman 2024):
- **Noise 35 %** — confidentiality is compliance, not comfort. No margin for failure.
- **Quiet rooms 40 %** — the brief must over-supply them for 3–6 h reading blocks.
- **Temperature 41 %** — the dual south/north façade will produce asymmetric loads; occupant-zone controls are the lever.
- **Plants 47 %** — a moderate planting tier (3–5 % of floor) sets the office above benchmark without clashing with a conservative aesthetic.
- **Informal unplanned meetings 52 %** / **idea-sharing 73 %** — in a hierarchical-collegiate culture, these translate to partner–associate corridors and library-edge encounters, not to open breakout.

Flex-ratio context (2024–2025): 69 % of organisations desk-share >40 % of population; legal-sector median 0.50–0.65 seats/FTE for associates and below, partners assigned. Altamont & Rees lands at **~1.0 across the board** — a deliberate counter-trend driven by 5/0 attendance and confidentiality, not by inertia.

Sources: `design://office-programming`, `design://flex-ratios`, `design://biophilic-office`, Leesman 2024, FlexOS 2024, Gensler GWS 2024.

## 5. Regulatory envelope

**Jurisdiction flag `[TO VERIFY]`**: the site is in London (UK), so the binding texts are *Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005*, *Approved Documents B / M / K*, *Equality Act 2010* and the *Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992*. The memo below uses French ERP/PMR/code du travail thresholds as a programming reference (orders of magnitude are comparable); each article must be transposed to its UK equivalent before submission.

- **Fire safety (ERP type W analogue / ADB)**: total occupancy ~160 FTE + visitors likely keeps the building in the lower risk category. Each floor needs ≥ 2 independent protected exits, travel distance ≤ 40 m (≤ 30 m in dead-ends). The **central stair** between floors must be enclosed (EI 30–60 self-closing doors) unless a derogation is obtained — arbitrate early if an open "design" stair is the brand gesture. Tasting kitchen to be sized below the commercial-kitchen threshold where possible. Library and archive fire-load `[TO VERIFY]`.
- **Accessibility (PMR / ADM / Equality Act)**: the discreet client path must remain **fully step-free and 1.40 m wide**, with a 1.50 m turning circle at each terminus and accessible reception counter (0.80 m segment, knee clearance). **Lift mandatory** between the two floors. At least one accessible WC per floor; preferably one per gendered block. Accessible doors ≥ 0.90 m clear (≥ 1.40 m for rooms that seat ≥ 100).
- **Workplace ergonomics (code du travail / WHSW 1992)**: general offices 120 lux legal floor, internal rooms 200 lux; best-practice EN 12464-1:2021 targets 500 lux on reading/screen tasks, 500 lux in boardrooms, 500 lux on library reading tables, 200 lux in archives, UGR ≤ 19 throughout. Partner offices on façade; archives, cellar, boardrooms on core.
- **Climate / envelope**: south (courtyard) façade needs solar control; north (Gracechurch Street) façade needs perimeter heating and acoustic attenuation against street noise. Occupant-zone temperature control is the Leesman lever.

## 6. Risks, assumptions, [TO VERIFY]

- **Envelope breach ~90 m²**. Levers: (a) reduce partner offices 14 → 12 m² (–40 m²); (b) drop boardrooms from 3 to 2, convert third to medium room (–20 m²); (c) revisit café sizing if an external canteen exists. Requires client arbitration.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Named-partner count** (assumed 20 of 110 lawyers). If actual is 25–30, partner-office surface jumps +70 to +140 m² and the breach becomes severe — the single biggest programme sensitivity.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Archive volume at 24 months** — 40 m² is a placeholder; a 120-lawyer City firm may need 60–100 m².
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Wine cellar + tasting kitchen (65 m², ~3 % of NIA)** are brand/entertainment spend, not productivity spend. Confirm priority against a second boardroom or a larger library.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Partner / associate attendance**: 5/0 is stated, but informal drift to 4/1 is common in City legal; enclosed offices will be underused on Fridays — write-off accepted for confidentiality.
- **Collaboration share at 11 %** is intentional but below benchmark (20–30 %). Mitigate with informal-collab islands inside paralegal neighbourhoods and at the library edge (not separate programme rows).
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Open vs enclosed central stair** — fire-enclosure default is conforming and economical; an open sculptural stair needs a derogation and a reinforced SSI/fire-engineered solution.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **UK regulatory transposition** of every ERP/PMR/code du travail reference before any permit submission.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Commercial-kitchen threshold** triggered by the tasting kitchen's installed power.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **PA allocation** (dedicated to partners vs pool).
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Reception brand requirements** (materiality, security screening, visitor-management flow).

**Conflicts resolved**: the Benchmarks memo suggested individual work should sit at the top of the 45–55 % band (~55 %) and amenity at 22–25 %; the Effectifs table lands at 62 % / 17 %. Kept the Effectifs figures because they flow from the brief's cellular constraints, and flagged the deviation from benchmark as intentional. The Benchmarks memo also anticipated 2 400–2 880 m² programme surface; the Effectifs table confirmed 2 489 m² — consistent.

## 7. Next steps

- **Upload the existing plateau plans** (both floors) to run a test-fit and confirm the 2 489 m² programme fits after real circulation and core deductions.
- **Confirm named-partner count and PA allocation** with the managing partner — this unlocks partner-office sizing and resolves the single biggest envelope risk.
- **Arbitrate the three value-engineering levers** (partner-office size, third boardroom, café size) with the client executive committee to close the 90 m² gap.
- **Confirm jurisdiction and appoint a UK Approved Inspector / fire engineer** to transpose the ERP / PMR / code du travail items to ADB/ADM/Equality Act before RIBA Stage 3.
- **Validate archive volume and library fire-load** with the firm's knowledge-management lead.

## 8. Sources

- `design://office-programming` — NIA/workstation ranges, programme split, meeting-room mix per 100 FTE, Leesman 2024 Lmi / H-Lmi and satisfaction percentages.
- `design://flex-ratios` — desk-sharing industry state 2024–2025, sector medians (legal/consulting 0.50–0.65), presence-policy → seats/FTE mapping, utilisation history.
- `design://biophilic-office` — moderate planting tier (3–5 % of floor area).
- `design://collaboration-spaces` — medium-room envelopes and meeting-room sizing references.
- https://www.leesmanindex.com/articles/redefining-the-workplace-why-employee-experience-matters/ — Leesman 2024 Lmi = 69.5, H-Lmi = 79.5; noise 35 %, quiet rooms 40 %, temperature 41 %, plants 47 %, informal meetings 52 %, idea-sharing 73 %.
- https://www.flexos.work/learn/the-optimal-desk-sharing-ratio-for-your-hybrid-office — 69 % of organisations desk-share >40 % of population; ratio distribution 2024–2025.
- https://www.gensler.com/gri/global-workplace-survey-2024 — 16 040 respondents, 15 countries; "workplace must beat home" evidence.
- Regulatory references (to be transposed to UK): French ERP type W (art. CO 35–CO 49, DH 1–DH 44, GC), arrêté PMR du 20 avril 2017, code du travail R. 4222 / R. 4223, EN 12464-1:2021.
- UK equivalents to confirm: Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, Approved Documents B / M / K, Equality Act 2010, Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992.

trace.name: Effectifs
trace.text:
### 1. Programme table

Headcount at 24-month horizon: **160 FTE** (partners + associates ≈ 110, paralegals 30, support 20). Attendance is 5/0 for lawyers/associates and 4/1 for paralegals — this is a **near-full-onsite** legal brief, not hybrid. The client explicitly rejects open-plan ("zero open-plan exposure of confidential matter") and mandates private partner offices + 2-to-a-room associates + paralegal neighbourhoods. Seat count is therefore driven by **typology**, not flex ratio.

Seat allocation:
- Named partners (assume ~20 of 110 lawyers as equity/named — `[À VÉRIFIER: ratio partners / associates exact]`) → **20 private offices**
- Associates ≈ 90 → 2-to-a-room → **45 shared offices**
- Paralegals 30 at 4/1 (avg presence 0.80, peak factor 1.15 per `flex_ratio_by_policy.4_1_hybrid_light`) → peak = 30 × 0.80 × 1.15 ≈ 28 → round to **30 seats** in neighbourhoods (ratio ~1.0, preserves identity)
- Support 20 (5/0) → **20 seats**

Sector benchmark `sector_flex_ratio_medians.legal_consulting` (0.50–0.65 for associates, partners assigned) is not applied: the brief overrides with an explicit 5/0 policy and assigned-seat culture.

| Typology | Count | Unit surface (m²) | Total (m²) | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Private partner office | 20 | 14 | 280 | Brief: "named partners have private offices"; 14 m² is mid-range single enclosed office for senior legal (supports desk + 2 guest chairs + bookcase). `[À VÉRIFIER: nombre exact de named partners]` |
| Shared associate office (2p) | 45 | 18 | 810 | Brief: "associates share 2-to-a-room"; 18 m² = 2 desks + small meeting corner, consistent with `collaboration-spaces §3` medium-room envelope scaled for dual workstation. 90 associates / 2. |
| Paralegal neighbourhood seat | 30 | 8 | 240 | Brief: "paralegals in neighbourhoods"; 4/1 policy → `flex_ratio_by_policy.4_1_hybrid_light` gives 0.90–0.95 seats/FTE but we keep 1.0 to avoid confidential-matter exposure at hot desks. 8 m²/seat is dense-but-not-open because neighbourhoods stay enclosed/semi-enclosed per brief. |
| Support / secretarial workstation | 20 | 9 | 180 | 20 support staff at 5/0; 9 m² per seat in semi-enclosed clusters adjacent to partner offices (PA pattern). `[À VÉRIFIER: répartition PA dédiées vs pool]` |
| Focus room (1–2p) | 6 | 5 | 30 | `meeting_room_mix_per_100_fte.focus_room_1_to_2p` = 2–4 / 100 FTE → 3–6 at 160 FTE. Take **top of range** (6): brief emphasises "deep reading in 3–6h blocks" — this is the dominant work mode. |
| Phone booth | 10 | 1.5 | 15 | `meeting_room_mix_per_100_fte.phone_booth_1p` = 6–10 / 100 FTE → 10–16; take **low end (10)** because most confidential calls happen inside private/shared offices, not booths. Booths serve paralegals and visiting counsel. |
| Huddle room (2–4p) | 6 | 8 | 48 | `meeting_room_mix_per_100_fte.huddle_2_to_4p` = 3–5 / 100 FTE → 5–8; take 6 for case-team stand-ups. Fully enclosed with DnT,A ≥ 40 dB per brief's confidentiality ask. |
| Medium meeting room (6–8p) | 5 | 22 | 110 | `meeting_room_mix_per_100_fte.medium_6_to_8p` = 2–4 / 100 FTE → 3–6; take 5 because legal case-team meetings are frequent and confidential. |
| Boardroom / deposition room | 3 | 40 | 120 | `meeting_room_mix_per_100_fte.large_10_to_14p` = 1–2 / 100 FTE → 2–3. Brief: "depositions in boardrooms" (plural) — take top of range (3) with premium finishes, DnT,A ≥ 45 dB. |
| Client reception / waiting | 1 | 60 | 60 | Brief: "client-facing reception" + "discreet client path from reception to partners" — needs waiting lounge separate from staff entry. Sized for 8–10 seated visitors + concierge. `[À VÉRIFIER: exigences image de marque de cabinet]` |
| Library (bound reports) | 1 | 80 | 80 | Brief: "library with bound reports". Sized for ~400 linear metres of shelving + 6 reading seats. Deep-reading anchor that reinforces brand. |
| Wine cellar | 1 | 15 | 15 | Brief: "wine cellar … for client dinners". Climate-controlled, off client path. `[À VÉRIFIER: capacité bouteilles cible]` |
| Tasting kitchen / private dining | 1 | 50 | 50 | Brief: "tasting kitchen for client dinners". Seats 10–14 at a dining table + open kitchen + sommelier station. Adjacent to wine cellar. |
| Café / staff restaurant | 1 | 120 | 120 | 160 FTE × 5/0 policy → lunchtime capacity critical. No external canteen assumed in City brief. ~0.75 m²/FTE at ~50 % peak sitting. `[À VÉRIFIER: cantine externe ou self-catering]` |
| Town hall (convertible café) | — | — | — | Brief has no all-hands requirement; café doubles for firm-wide events. `meeting_room_mix.town_hall_40p_plus` = 0–1. |
| Print / copy / secure doc room | 3 | 10 | 30 | 1 per floor + 1 secure document room with shredder and locked output tray (confidentiality constraint). |
| Locker bank | — | — | 10 | Only paralegals flex (30 lockers at ~0.3 m²/10 lockers per `flex-ratios §8.2`) = ~9 m². Lawyers use their offices. |
| Reception support (cloakroom, coats) | 1 | 15 | 15 | Client path — coat storage for visitors + staff cloakroom. |
| Back-office / MDF / IT | 2 | 20 | 40 | One per floor: IT racks, patching, UPS. Legal firms need secure comms room (document matter management). |
| Archives / strongroom | 1 | 40 | 40 | Bound-report + closed-matter archives; fire-rated, access-controlled. Separate from library (active reference). `[À VÉRIFIER: volume d'archives à 24 mois]` |
| Storage / cleaning / utility | 2 | 10 | 20 | 1 per floor. |
| PMR WC + standard WC blocks | 2 | 25 | 50 | 1 block per floor, each with adapted WC per `pmr_critical_widths_m.adapted_wc_internal_min_m` (1.50 × 2.10). |

**Programme sub-total (before circulation): 2 263 m²**

Circulation inside NIA at lower end of `program_split_percent_of_nia.circulation` (10 %, because the plan is dominated by enclosed rooms which already absorb intra-room circulation): ~226 m². The closed-cell typology mechanically eats circulation via corridors → likely closer to **14–15 %** in reality.

**Programme total with circulation: ~2 489 m²** (envelope: 2 400 m² usable).

### 2. Surface summary

- **Total programme surface**: ~2 489 m² (sum of table + 10 % circulation) vs **2 400 m² available** → **overshoots the envelope by ~90 m² (3.7 %)**. This is a red flag, not a showstopper — but it confirms the programme is tight and must be value-engineered below (see Risks).
- **Split across the four categories** of `program_split_percent_of_nia`:
  - Individual work (partner offices + associate offices + paralegal seats + support + focus + phone booth): **1 555 m² = 62 %** — above the 45–55 % band, driven by the cellular legal typology.
  - Collaboration (huddle + medium + boardrooms): **278 m² = 11 %** — well below the 20–30 % band, because case-team meetings happen inside lawyer offices.
  - Support & amenity (reception, library, cellar, dining, café, print, lockers, archives, BO, WC): **430 m² = 17 %** — within the 15–25 % band.
  - Circulation: **~226 m² = 10 %** — at the low end; realistic corridor count will push this up.
- **Implied NIA per FTE at 24-month horizon**: 2 400 / 160 = **15.0 m²/FTE** — at the top of `nia_per_workstation_m2.activity_based_hybrid_2024` (12–15) and entering `premium_hq` territory (15–18). Consistent with a City law firm; below this ratio the programme simply does not fit.

### 3. Risks and assumptions

- **Envelope breach (~90 m²)**: the programme overshoots 2 400 m². Two likely value-engineering levers: (a) reduce private partner offices from 14 m² to 12 m² (–40 m²) `[À VÉRIFIER auprès des partners nommés: acceptabilité]`; (b) reduce boardrooms from 3 to 2 and convert the third to flexible medium room (–20 m²). Decision needs client arbitration.
- **Partner / associate ratio `[À VÉRIFIER]`**: I assumed 20 named partners out of 110 lawyers. If the real count is 25–30, the partner-office surface jumps by 70–140 m² and the envelope breach becomes severe. This is the single biggest programme sensitivity.
- **Attendance policy tension**: the brief states 5/0 for lawyers but post-COVID City legal practice commonly drifts to 4/1 informally. Sizing lawyer offices at full assigned count is the safe choice, but flag that if partners start working from home one day/week the office surfaces 20 + 45 = 65 enclosed rooms underused on Fridays — this is a **write-off accepted** for confidentiality, per brief.
- **Collaboration share (11 %) is below the 20–30 % band**: this is intentional given the cellular typology, but it means **informal unplanned meetings** (Leesman 52 % driver) will suffer. Mitigation: embed informal-collab islands inside paralegal neighbourhoods and near the library, not captured as separate programme rows.
- **Wine cellar + tasting kitchen (65 m²)**: these are brand/amenity moves consuming ~3 % of NIA. They are not "programmatic" in the Leesman sense and their ROI is client-entertainment, not productivity. Confirm the client accepts this trade-off vs. adding a second boardroom or a larger library `[À VÉRIFIER: priorisation client]`.
- **Archives sizing `[À VÉRIFIER]`**: 40 m² is a placeholder. A 120-lawyer City firm accumulates significant closed-matter paper; the real figure may be 60–100 m², which would push the envelope breach past 150 m² and force harder choices.

trace.name: Benchmarks
trace.text:
# Benchmarks memo — Altamont & Rees

### 1. Employee-experience baseline

The 2024 Leesman global office score is **Lmi = 69.5**, sitting just at the Leesman+ "Excellent" threshold (70.0). Home-working scores **H-Lmi = 79.5**, a persistent **10-point gap** (`design://office-programming §5`). For a 5-days-on-site partner/associate policy, this gap is the single most important number in the brief: if the office doesn't beat the home study on focus, acoustic control and comfort, attendance will be resented rather than embraced.

Against the pain points relevant to Altamont & Rees (Leesman 2024, % satisfied):

- **Noise levels: 35 %** (`design://office-programming §5`) — directly relevant to the "zero open-plan exposure of confidential matter" ask. The global panel is failing on this; a legal fit-out has no margin to fail on it, since confidentiality is not comfort, it is compliance.
- **Quiet rooms: 40 %** (`design://office-programming §5`) — deep reading in 3–6 h blocks is the dominant associate/paralegal work mode. Benchmark says most offices under-supply quiet rooms; the brief must over-supply them.
- **Temperature control: 41 %** (`design://office-programming §5`) — dual-façade plate (south courtyard / north Gracechurch) will produce asymmetric loads; occupant-zone controls are the lever to beat the benchmark.
- **Plants and greenery: 47 %** (`design://office-programming §5`) — modest baseline. A moderate planting tier (3–5 % floor area, `design://biophilic-office §1.1`) sets the office above benchmark without conflict with a conservative law-firm aesthetic.
- **Informal unplanned meetings: 52 % value them** and **73 % say the office supports idea-sharing** (`design://office-programming §5`) — the positive anchors. In a hierarchical/collegiate culture, these translate to partner–associate corridors and library-edge encounters, not to open breakout.

### 2. Flex-ratio benchmarks

Current industry state (2024–2025, `design://flex-ratios §4.1`):

- **69 %** of client organisations have **> 40 %** of population desk-sharing (FlexOS 2024).
- **48 %** of organisations target a **1.01 – 1.49 pers/seat** ratio in 2025, up from 21 % in 2024.
- Ratios **≥ 1.5 pers/seat** grew **+93 % since 2023**.
- Dedicated desks fell from **51 % (2021) → 40 % (2024)**.
- Global office utilisation: **41 % (2023) → 49 % (2024) → 54 % (2025)**, vs **61 %** pre-pandemic baseline.

**Sector reference — Legal / consulting: 0.50 – 0.65 seats/FTE for associates and below, partners assigned** (`design://flex-ratios §4.3`).

**Positioning for Altamont & Rees**: the brief points hard in the opposite direction of the industry drift. Policy is **5 days on-site for partners and associates, 4 days for paralegals** — a 5/0 to 4/1 regime, which maps to **≥ 1.0 seats/FTE for partners+associates** and **0.90 – 0.95 for paralegals** (`design://flex-ratios §5.1`). Combined with named-partner offices, 2-to-a-room associates, and confidentiality constraints, the defensible target is **assigned seating throughout, ratio ≈ 1.0**. At 160 FTE horizon this means ~160 seats, against the industry median legal benchmark of ~80–100. This is a deliberate counter-trend position and should be argued as such, not apologised for.

### 3. Programme-split benchmark

The Effectifs Agent has not returned its output; the reference ranges (`design://office-programming §2`) are:

| Programme zone              | Industry range (% NIA) |
|-----------------------------|------------------------|
| Individual work             | 45 – 55 %              |
| Collaboration               | 20 – 30 %              |
| Support & amenities         | 15 – 25 %              |
| Circulation inside NIA      | 10 – 15 %              |

**Calibration for this brief**:
- **Individual work** should sit at the **top** of the range (≈ 55 %): partner offices, 2-person associate rooms, paralegal neighbourhoods, library reading positions — the dominant declared mode is 3–6 h deep reading.
- **Collaboration** sits at the **low-mid** of the range (≈ 20 %) but is skewed toward **enclosed boardrooms** (depositions, client dinners) rather than open huddle; the standard meeting-mix heuristic (`design://office-programming §3`) of 1–2 large/boardrooms per 100 pax must be **doubled or tripled** for this profile.
- **Support & amenities** should sit at the **top** of the range (≈ 22–25 %) to accommodate the wine cellar, tasting kitchen, client-path reception, and library of bound reports — all explicitly named in the brief and all above the "default" amenity envelope.
- **Circulation** must carry the **discreet client path** requirement — plan at the **top** of the range (13–15 %) because the client flow from reception to partner offices cannot pass through the associate/paralegal working core.

**NIA/workstation** (`design://office-programming §1`): a legal fit-out with private offices and boardrooms sits in **Premium HQ, 15–18 m²/workstation**. At 160 FTE this implies 2 400 – 2 880 m², which maps almost exactly onto the available 2 400 m² — the plate is **tight for the programme**, and the collaboration/amenity asks will compete hard against individual work. Flag for the programming phase.

### 4. Sources

- `design://office-programming` — NIA/workstation ranges, programme split, meeting-room mix, Leesman 2024 Lmi/H-Lmi and satisfaction %.
- `design://flex-ratios` — desk-sharing industry state 2024–2025, sector medians (legal/consulting 0.50–0.65), presence-policy → seats/FTE mapping, utilisation history.
- `design://biophilic-office` — moderate planting tier (3–5 % of floor), defensible floor for a conservative aesthetic.
- https://www.leesmanindex.com/articles/redefining-the-workplace-why-employee-experience-matters/ — Leesman 2024 Lmi = 69.5, H-Lmi = 79.5, noise 35 %, quiet rooms 40 %, temperature 41 %, plants 47 %, informal meetings 52 %, idea-sharing 73 %.
- https://www.flexos.work/learn/the-optimal-desk-sharing-ratio-for-your-hybrid-office — 69 % of organisations >40 % desk-sharing, ratio distribution 2024–2025.
- https://www.gensler.com/gri/global-workplace-survey-2024 — 16 040 respondents, 15 countries; choice and premier-building findings anchoring the "workplace must beat home" argument.

trace.name: Contraintes
trace.text:
# Constraints memo — Altamont & Rees (City of London)

> **Préambule juridictionnel** `[À VÉRIFIER]` : le brief localise le site à Londres (UK). Les réglementations citées ci-dessous (ERP type W, arrêté PMR du 20 avril 2017, code du travail R. 4222 / R. 4223) sont **françaises** et ne s'appliquent pas de plein droit à un fit-out londonien, où s'appliquent plutôt *Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005*, *Approved Documents B / M / K*, *Equality Act 2010* et *Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992*. À clarifier avec le client **avant** tout test-fit : le mémo ci-dessous traite les exigences françaises comme référentiel de programmation (demande apparente du Design Office) ; les seuils restent comparables en ordre de grandeur, mais chaque article devra être transposé aux équivalents UK avant dépôt de permis.

---

## 1. ERP type W — sécurité incendie

### Contraintes applicables au brief

- **Catégorie ERP** : effectif déclaré 160 FTE à 24 mois, sans public permanent massif hors réception + salles de déposition. Effectif pub + staff vraisemblablement **< 300 → Catégorie 4**. Conséquence directe : **alarme type 4** (déclencheurs manuels + diffuseurs sonores), pas de SSI catégorie C/D/E imposé. C'est le seuil le moins contraignant ; toute dérive au-dessus de 300 bascule en catégorie 3 avec alarme type 3 → coût matériel supérieur.
- **Dégagements** : deux plateaux de ~1 200 m² reliés par escalier central. Chaque niveau doit disposer d'**au moins 2 sorties distinctes et protégées** (art. CO 35–CO 39). Pour une zone de ~80 personnes / étage (160 / 2), **2 UP (1,40 m)** par niveau minimum.
- **Distance à parcourir** : ≤ **40 m** depuis tout point vers la sortie la plus proche ; ≤ **30 m** en cul-de-sac `[À VÉRIFIER art. CO 49]`. Avec bureaux fermés périmétriques (partners) + bibliothèque + cave à vin en plan profond, risque élevé de culs-de-sac — à cartographier dès l'esquisse.
- **Escalier central** : le brief décrit un escalier reliant les deux niveaux. Tout escalier interne entre deux niveaux desservis doit être **encloisonné** (volume coupe-feu, portes EI 30 – EI 60 auto-fermantes). Un escalier « design » ouvert entre les deux plateaux nécessite une **dispensation réglementaire** du bureau de contrôle — à arbitrer tôt, car c'est un marqueur identitaire fréquent dans les cabinets d'avocats.
- **Désenfumage** : toute zone ouverte > 300 m² et toute circulation > 30 m de long doit être désenfumée `[À VÉRIFIER seuils DH 1 – DH 44]`. Le brief impose **« zero open-plan exposure »** — ce qui plaide naturellement pour des zones cloisonnées < 300 m², donc probablement hors seuil désenfumage mécanique. À confirmer sur la bibliothèque et la salle de dégustation.
- **Cave à vin + cuisine de dégustation** : risques spécifiques. La cuisine (y compris « tasting kitchen ») introduit un risque d'origine thermique qui relève des articles **GC** (grandes cuisines) ou, si puissance limitée, du régime office bureau standard `[À VÉRIFIER seuil puissance déclenchant GC]`. La cave (stockage alcool) n'est pas un local à risque au sens réglementaire si volumes raisonnables, mais à documenter.
- **Extincteurs** : ≥ 1 extincteur eau pulvérisée 6 L / 200 m² → minimum **12 unités** sur 2 400 m². Distance max à un extincteur **15 m**. CO₂ près des tableaux électriques et local serveur.
- **Finitions** : circulations murs/plafonds **B-s2, d0**, sols **Cfl-s1** ; pièces murs **C-s3, d0** ; textiles assises **M1 / B-s1, d0**. À inscrire au CCTP dès le Cat B.

### Actions de programmation à prendre maintenant

1. Cartographier les deux chemins d'évacuation indépendants par niveau **avant** de figer la position des bureaux de partners (risque qu'ils consomment un linéaire de façade empêchant la seconde issue).
2. Arbitrer avec le client : escalier encloisonné (conforme, économique) vs escalier ouvert sculptural (dispensation à obtenir + SSI renforcé).
3. Dimensionner cuisine de dégustation pour rester **sous le seuil GC** si possible — à cadrer avec le bureau de contrôle.
4. Confirmer que la bibliothèque (« bound reports ») n'est **pas** classée local à risques particuliers (charge calorifique du papier). Si densité de stockage élevée, prévoir cloisonnement CF 1h `[À VÉRIFIER]`.

### À vérifier

- `[À VÉRIFIER]` effectif exact déclaré une fois réception / salle de conseil / event room prises en compte — bascule possible 4 → 3.
- `[À VÉRIFIER art. exact]` seuil de puissance cuisine déclenchant le régime GC.
- `[À VÉRIFIER]` classement de la bibliothèque (local à risques courants ou particuliers selon charge calorifique).

---

## 2. PMR / accessibilité

### Contraintes applicables au brief

- **Parcours client discret** demandé par le brief : il doit rester **accessible PMR de bout en bout**. La discrétion ne peut pas se traduire par un cheminement étroit, en escalier, ou sans itinéraire alternatif accessible — principe d'autonomie (art. 1, arrêté 20 avril 2017).
- **Portes** : réception, salle du conseil (« boardroom » pour dépositions), et toute zone pouvant accueillir ≥ 100 personnes → portes **≥ 1,40 m utile**. Les salles de déposition elles-mêmes, de capacité limitée (10–20 pers.), restent à **0,90 m utile**.
- **Circulations** : principales **≥ 1,40 m**, secondaires **≥ 0,90 m** `[À VÉRIFIER valeurs exactes dans l'arrêté]`.
- **Aire de rotation 1,50 m** en début / fin de circulation, dans chaque cabinet PMR, et devant chaque position de réception.
- **Sanitaires** : au moins **1 cabinet PMR par bloc sanitaire** ouvert au public, dimensions internes ≥ 1,50 × 2,10 m, espace de transfert 0,80 × 1,30 m. Avec 160 FTE sur 2 niveaux, prévoir **a minima 1 cabinet PMR par niveau**, idéalement 1 par bloc sanitaire homme + 1 par bloc sanitaire femme `[À VÉRIFIER mutualisation autorisée]`.
- **Liaison inter-niveaux** : escalier central + **ascenseur PMR obligatoire** dès qu'un étage est desservi avec ≥ 50 personnes `[À VÉRIFIER seuil exact]`. Avec 160 FTE sur 2 plateaux, l'ascenseur est non négociable.
- **Réception adaptée** : partie du comptoir à **0,80 m**, dégagement genoux 0,70 × 0,60 × 0,70 m, aire d'approche **0,90 × 1,30 m** devant. C'est un point d'attention pour un cabinet haut de gamme : la réception adaptée doit être intégrée au design, pas rapportée.
- **Bibliothèque et cave à vin** : si ouvertes aux clients (parcours « client-facing »), accessibilité PMR complète exigée. Les rayonnages de « bound reports » doivent préserver une circulation **≥ 0,90 m** et une aire de rotation 1,50 m en fond.
- **Salle du conseil / déposition** : aire de rotation 1,50 m à l'intérieur, porte 0,90 m utile, aucun seuil > 2 cm.

### Actions de programmation à prendre maintenant

1. Tracer le **parcours client discret** et vérifier qu'il accepte un fauteuil roulant sur tout son linéaire (largeurs, seuils, rayon de giration à chaque coude).
2. Positionner **l'ascenseur PMR** dès le plan de masse — il structure la liaison entre les deux niveaux au même titre que l'escalier central.
3. Vérifier la compatibilité entre le principe « partners en bureau individuel périmétrique » et le besoin de circulation principale ≥ 1,40 m sans compromis.
4. Intégrer la réception adaptée dans le design du comptoir d'accueil (pas en appendice).
5. Au moins **1 sanitaire PMR par niveau** ; préférer 1 par bloc genré.

### À vérifier

- `[À VÉRIFIER]` largeur exacte corridor principal (1,40 m paraît standard mais l'arrêté doit être relu ligne à ligne).
- `[À VÉRIFIER]` seuil d'effectif à l'étage déclenchant l'ascenseur obligatoire (ici acquis de toute façon).
- `[À VÉRIFIER]` règles de mutualisation cabinet PMR entre blocs H/F.
- Statut ERP ou code du travail pur selon la zone (partie bureaux pure staff vs partie client-facing) — conditionne l'intensité des exigences PMR.

---

## 3. Code du travail — ergonomie

### Contraintes applicables au brief

- **Éclairage — plancher légal** (R. 4223-1 à R. 4223-12) :
  - Bureaux généraux : **120 lux minimum**
  - Bureau sans lumière naturelle : **200 lux minimum**
  - Circulations à occupation permanente : **60 lux** `[À VÉRIFIER]`
  - La lumière naturelle doit être priorisée (R. 4223-3). Conséquence programmatique : les bureaux de partners doivent être sur façade ; les espaces en cœur de plan (cave à vin, stockage bibliothèque, salles de déposition) entrent en catégorie « sans lumière naturelle » → 200 lux minimum.
- **Éclairage — bonne pratique EN 12464-1:2021** :
  - Lecture / rédaction / écran : **500 lux** maintenus sur zone de tâche
  - Salles de réunion et boardroom : **500 lux**
  - Réception : **300 lux** `[À VÉRIFIER]`
  - Archives / stockage : **200 lux**
  - Bibliothèque pour lecture soutenue (« 3–6h blocks ») : viser **500 lux** sur les tables de travail, 300 lux sur les rayonnages.
  - Majoration **+1 step** pour travailleurs 50+ ou tâches visuellement exigeantes → **750 lux** envisageable pour les postes de partners seniors.
  - **UGR ≤ 19** en

trace.name: Consolidator
trace.text:
# Functional programme — Altamont & Rees

## 1. Context and headcount

- **Client**: Altamont & Rees, commercial-law partnership, City of London (Gracechurch Street, two floors, south façade onto courtyard / north façade onto street).
- **Headcount**: 140 today (90 lawyers + 30 paralegals + 20 support) → **160 FTE at 24 months**. Assumed split at horizon: ~20 named partners, ~90 associates, 30 paralegals, 20 support `[TO VERIFY: exact partner / associate ratio]`.
- **Presence policy**: 5/0 for partners and associates, 4/1 for paralegals, 5/0 for support. This is a **near-full-onsite** legal brief, not a hybrid brief.
- **Flex-ratio policy**: assigned seating throughout, **ratio ≈ 1.0 seats/FTE**. This is a deliberate counter-trend position against the 2024–2025 industry drift (48 % of organisations now target 1.01–1.49 pers/seat, legal sector median 0.50–0.65) and should be argued as such, not apologised for.
- **Design intent retained from the brief**:
  - "Named partners have private offices" / "associates share 2-to-a-room" / "paralegals in neighbourhoods"
  - "Zero open-plan exposure of confidential matter"
  - "Discreet client path from reception to partners"
  - "Deep reading in 3–6 h blocks" as the dominant work mode
  - "Library with bound reports, wine cellar and tasting kitchen for client dinners"
  - "Depositions in boardrooms" (plural)

## 2. Functional programme

| Typology | Count | Unit (m²) | Total (m²) | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Private partner office | 20 | 14 | 280 | Brief: named partners in private offices. 14 m² supports desk + 2 guest chairs + bookcase. `[TO VERIFY: exact named-partner count]` |
| Shared associate office (2p) | 45 | 18 | 810 | Brief: associates 2-to-a-room. 90 associates ÷ 2. |
| Paralegal neighbourhood seat | 30 | 8 | 240 | Brief: paralegals in neighbourhoods. 4/1 policy would give 0.90–0.95 seats/FTE, but held at 1.0 to avoid confidential-matter exposure at hot desks. |
| Support / secretarial workstation | 20 | 9 | 180 | 20 support, 5/0, semi-enclosed clusters adjacent to partner offices (PA pattern). `[TO VERIFY: dedicated PAs vs pool]` |
| Focus room (1–2p) | 6 | 5 | 30 | Top of 2–4 / 100 FTE band; deep-reading is the dominant declared mode. |
| Phone booth | 10 | 1.5 | 15 | Low end of 6–10 / 100 FTE; most confidential calls happen inside offices. |
| Huddle room (2–4p) | 6 | 8 | 48 | Case-team stand-ups, fully enclosed, DnT,A ≥ 40 dB. |
| Medium meeting room (6–8p) | 5 | 22 | 110 | Frequent confidential case-team meetings. |
| Boardroom / deposition room | 3 | 40 | 120 | Brief: depositions in boardrooms (plural); top of 1–2 / 100 FTE band, DnT,A ≥ 45 dB. |
| Client reception / waiting | 1 | 60 | 60 | Client-facing reception + discreet path; 8–10 seated visitors + concierge. |
| Library (bound reports) | 1 | 80 | 80 | ~400 linear m of shelving + 6 reading seats. |
| Wine cellar | 1 | 15 | 15 | Climate-controlled, off client path. `[TO VERIFY: target bottle capacity]` |
| Tasting kitchen / private dining | 1 | 50 | 50 | Seats 10–14 + open kitchen + sommelier station, adjacent to cellar. |
| Café / staff restaurant | 1 | 120 | 120 | 160 FTE at 5/0; ~0.75 m²/FTE at 50 % peak. `[TO VERIFY: external canteen option]` |
| Print / copy / secure doc room | 3 | 10 | 30 | 1 per floor + 1 secure doc room with shredder, locked output tray. |
| Locker bank | — | — | 10 | Paralegals only (30 lockers). |
| Reception cloakroom | 1 | 15 | 15 | Visitor + staff coats. |
| Back-office / MDF / IT | 2 | 20 | 40 | One per floor; secure comms for matter management. |
| Archives / strongroom | 1 | 40 | 40 | Fire-rated, access-controlled; separate from active library. `[TO VERIFY: 24-month archive volume — could push to 60–100 m²]` |
| Storage / cleaning / utility | 2 | 10 | 20 | 1 per floor. |
| PMR / accessible WC + standard WC | 2 | 25 | 50 | 1 block per floor; accessible WC 1.50 × 2.10 m minimum. |

**Sub-total (before circulation): 2 263 m²**
Circulation at 10 %: +226 m²
**Programme total: ~2 489 m²** vs **2 400 m² available → overshoots by ~90 m² (3.7 %)**.

## 3. Surface summary

- **Total programme surface**: **~2 489 m²** against **2 400 m²** usable → envelope breach **~90 m²**.
- **Split across the four categories**:
  - Individual work: **1 555 m² = 62 %** (above the 45–55 % band, driven by cellular legal typology)
  - Collaboration: **278 m² = 11 %** (below the 20–30 % band; meetings happen inside lawyer offices)
  - Support & amenity: **430 m² = 17 %** (within the 15–25 % band)
  - Circulation: **~226 m² = 10 %** (at the low end; cellular plans typically land at 14–15 %)
- **Implied NIA/FTE at 24-month horizon**: **15.0 m²/FTE** — top of the activity-based band (12–15) and entering premium-HQ territory (15–18). Consistent with a City law firm; below this ratio the programme does not fit.
- **Envelope check**: tight. The programme must be value-engineered by ~90 m² (see §6).

## 4. Industry positioning

Leesman 2024 baseline: **Lmi = 69.5**, home score **H-Lmi = 79.5** — a persistent 10-point gap. For a 5-days-on-site lawyer policy, the office must beat the home study on focus, acoustics and comfort, or attendance will be resented.

Relevant pain points (% satisfied, Leesman 2024):
- **Noise 35 %** — confidentiality is compliance, not comfort. No margin for failure.
- **Quiet rooms 40 %** — the brief must over-supply them for 3–6 h reading blocks.
- **Temperature 41 %** — the dual south/north façade will produce asymmetric loads; occupant-zone controls are the lever.
- **Plants 47 %** — a moderate planting tier (3–5 % of floor) sets the office above benchmark without clashing with a conservative aesthetic.
- **Informal unplanned meetings 52 %** / **idea-sharing 73 %** — in a hierarchical-collegiate culture, these translate to partner–associate corridors and library-edge encounters, not to open breakout.

Flex-ratio context (2024–2025): 69 % of organisations desk-share >40 % of population; legal-sector median 0.50–0.65 seats/FTE for associates and below, partners assigned. Altamont & Rees lands at **~1.0 across the board** — a deliberate counter-trend driven by 5/0 attendance and confidentiality, not by inertia.

Sources: `design://office-programming`, `design://flex-ratios`, `design://biophilic-office`, Leesman 2024, FlexOS 2024, Gensler GWS 2024.

## 5. Regulatory envelope

**Jurisdiction flag `[TO VERIFY]`**: the site is in London (UK), so the binding texts are *Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005*, *Approved Documents B / M / K*, *Equality Act 2010* and the *Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992*. The memo below uses French ERP/PMR/code du travail thresholds as a programming reference (orders of magnitude are comparable); each article must be transposed to its UK equivalent before submission.

- **Fire safety (ERP type W analogue / ADB)**: total occupancy ~160 FTE + visitors likely keeps the building in the lower risk category. Each floor needs ≥ 2 independent protected exits, travel distance ≤ 40 m (≤ 30 m in dead-ends). The **central stair** between floors must be enclosed (EI 30–60 self-closing doors) unless a derogation is obtained — arbitrate early if an open "design" stair is the brand gesture. Tasting kitchen to be sized below the commercial-kitchen threshold where possible. Library and archive fire-load `[TO VERIFY]`.
- **Accessibility (PMR / ADM / Equality Act)**: the discreet client path must remain **fully step-free and 1.40 m wide**, with a 1.50 m turning circle at each terminus and accessible reception counter (0.80 m segment, knee clearance). **Lift mandatory** between the two floors. At least one accessible WC per floor; preferably one per gendered block. Accessible doors ≥ 0.90 m clear (≥ 1.40 m for rooms that seat ≥ 100).
- **Workplace ergonomics (code du travail / WHSW 1992)**: general offices 120 lux legal floor, internal rooms 200 lux; best-practice EN 12464-1:2021 targets 500 lux on reading/screen tasks, 500 lux in boardrooms, 500 lux on library reading tables, 200 lux in archives, UGR ≤ 19 throughout. Partner offices on façade; archives, cellar, boardrooms on core.
- **Climate / envelope**: south (courtyard) façade needs solar control; north (Gracechurch Street) façade needs perimeter heating and acoustic attenuation against street noise. Occupant-zone temperature control is the Leesman lever.

## 6. Risks, assumptions, [TO VERIFY]

- **Envelope breach ~90 m²**. Levers: (a) reduce partner offices 14 → 12 m² (–40 m²); (b) drop boardrooms from 3 to 2, convert third to medium room (–20 m²); (c) revisit café sizing if an external canteen exists. Requires client arbitration.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Named-partner count** (assumed 20 of 110 lawyers). If actual is 25–30, partner-office surface jumps +70 to +140 m² and the breach becomes severe — the single biggest programme sensitivity.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Archive volume at 24 months** — 40 m² is a placeholder; a 120-lawyer City firm may need 60–100 m².
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Wine cellar + tasting kitchen (65 m², ~3 % of NIA)** are brand/entertainment spend, not productivity spend. Confirm priority against a second boardroom or a larger library.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Partner / associate attendance**: 5/0 is stated, but informal drift to 4/1 is common in City legal; enclosed offices will be underused on Fridays — write-off accepted for confidentiality.
- **Collaboration share at 11 %** is intentional but below benchmark (20–30 %). Mitigate with informal-collab islands inside paralegal neighbourhoods and at the library edge (not separate programme rows).
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Open vs enclosed central stair** — fire-enclosure default is conforming and economical; an open sculptural stair needs a derogation and a reinforced SSI/fire-engineered solution.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **UK regulatory transposition** of every ERP/PMR/code du travail reference before any permit submission.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Commercial-kitchen threshold** triggered by the tasting kitchen's installed power.
- `[TO VERIFY]` **PA allocation** (dedicated to partners vs pool).
- `[TO VERIFY]` **Reception brand requirements** (materiality, security screening, visitor-management flow).

**Conflicts resolved**: the Benchmarks memo suggested individual work should sit at the top of the 45–55 % band (~55 %) and amenity at 22–25 %; the Effectifs table lands at 62 % / 17 %. Kept the Effectifs figures because they flow from the brief's cellular constraints, and flagged the deviation from benchmark as intentional. The Benchmarks memo also anticipated 2 400–2 880 m² programme surface; the Effectifs table confirmed 2 489 m² — consistent.

## 7. Next steps

- **Upload the existing plateau plans** (both floors) to run a test-fit and confirm the 2 489 m² programme fits after real circulation and core deductions.
- **Confirm named-partner count and PA allocation** with the managing partner — this unlocks partner-office sizing and resolves the single biggest envelope risk.
- **Arbitrate the three value-engineering levers** (partner-office size, third boardroom, café size) with the client executive committee to close the 90 m² gap.
- **Confirm jurisdiction and appoint a UK Approved Inspector / fire engineer** to transpose the ERP / PMR / code du travail items to ADB/ADM/Equality Act before RIBA Stage 3.
- **Validate archive volume and library fire-load** with the firm's knowledge-management lead.

## 8. Sources

- `design://office-programming` — NIA/workstation ranges, programme split, meeting-room mix per 100 FTE, Leesman 2024 Lmi / H-Lmi and satisfaction percentages.
- `design://flex-ratios` — desk-sharing industry state 2024–2025, sector medians (legal/consulting 0.50–0.65), presence-policy → seats/FTE mapping, utilisation history.
- `design://biophilic-office` — moderate planting tier (3–5 % of floor area).
- `design://collaboration-spaces` — medium-room envelopes and meeting-room sizing references.
- https://www.leesmanindex.com/articles/redefining-the-workplace-why-employee-experience-matters/ — Leesman 2024 Lmi = 69.5, H-Lmi = 79.5; noise 35 %, quiet rooms 40 %, temperature 41 %, plants 47 %, informal meetings 52 %, idea-sharing 73 %.
- https://www.flexos.work/learn/the-optimal-desk-sharing-ratio-for-your-hybrid-office — 69 % of organisations desk-share >40 % of population; ratio distribution 2024–2025.
- https://www.gensler.com/gri/global-workplace-survey-2024 — 16 040 respondents, 15 countries; "workplace must beat home" evidence.
- Regulatory references (to be transposed to UK): French ERP type W (art. CO 35–CO 49, DH 1–DH 44, GC), arrêté PMR du 20 avril 2017, code du travail R. 4222 / R. 4223, EN 12464-1:2021.
- UK equivalents to confirm: Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, Approved Documents B / M / K, Equality Act 2010, Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992.
